title_en: Europe’s economy is a mess. Its stock markets are a steal
title_zh: 欧洲经济乱象丛生，却有着“捡漏级”股市
rubric_zh: 国际投资者依然缺乏兴趣

paragraphs.en: “The stock MARKET is not the economy” might be a cliché, but it is still jarring to see quite how far the two can part ways. Surely economic growth should be good for shareholders? Yet study after study has suggested the opposite. One by Jay Ritter of the University of Florida, for instance, compared the returns on 16 countries’ stock markets with their GDP growth per person between 1900 and 2002. The two appeared, if anything, to be negatively correlated—meaning that the faster a country became richer, the worse its investors tended to do.
paragraphs.zh: “股市不是经济”也许是个陈词滥调，但看到两者竟能如此背离仍令人震惊。经济增长难道不该对股东有利吗？然而一项又一项研究却指向相反方向。例如，佛罗里达大学的杰伊·里特（Jay Ritter）比较了16个国家在1900年至2002年间的股市回报率与人均GDP增长。研究结果表明，两者似乎至少没有正相关，甚至是负相关——也就是说，一个国家越富裕，投资者的表现往往越差。

paragraphs.en: Mr Ritter’s results are worth bearing in mind when contemplating some of the world’s most underloved shares. Brief flirtations aside, it has been a long time since Europe’s big markets have thrilled international investors, and there are no prizes for guessing why. The IMF reckons GDP growth in the euro area will be just 1.1% this year, compared with 1.8% for advanced economies the world over and 2.3% for America. Some of Europe’s peripheral and emerging bourses are buzzing; the mainstays are not.
paragraphs.zh: 在思考世界上那些最不受欢迎的股票时，里特的结论值得牢记。除去短暂的热潮，欧洲的主要股市很久以来都没能让国际投资者感到兴奋，而这为何如此几乎无需猜测。国际货币基金组织（IMF）预计，欧元区今年GDP增长率仅为1.1%，而全球发达经济体为1.8%，美国为2.3%。欧洲一些外围和新兴市场的交易所生机盎然，主流市场却没有这种活力。

paragraphs.en: Worse, Europe appears increasingly ill-equipped for the 21st century. It has neither artificial-intelligence giants to compete with those in America and China nor enough data centres to host their models. Its electricity grid is strained even without them. So is its power supply, since Europeans import nearly 60% of their energy—a vulnerability laid bare by wars in Ukraine and Iran.
paragraphs.zh: 更糟的是，欧洲似乎越来越不适应21世纪。与美国和中国相比，它既没有可与之竞争的人工智能巨头，也没有足够的数据中心来托管这些模型。即使在没有这些模型的情况下，其电网也已承受很大压力；能源供应也同样紧张，因为欧洲几乎要进口60%的能源——这在乌克兰和伊朗的战争中被彻底暴露了它的脆弱性。

paragraphs.en: In spite of all this, European shares deserve a lot more affection than they are getting. More than anywhere else in the world, the old continent’s stock markets are not its economy. Even as GDP growth languishes, their fundamentals look surprisingly good.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，欧洲股市依然值得获得比现在多得多的关注。比起世界上任何地方，欧洲这个古老大陆的股市都不是其经济本身。即便GDP增长停滞不前，它们的基本面仍然出人意料地稳健。

paragraphs.en: For a case study, consider what happened after the Iran war closed the Strait of Hormuz. Until then, one European equity strategist says, she had spent much of her time fielding calls from international investors who worried they were overexposed to America and wanted to diversify. Capital was flowing into European equity funds more quickly than it had been for some time. When the bombs began to fall, the phone calls stopped and the capital flows all but dried up. Share prices tanked pretty much everywhere, but those of European firms then recovered far slower than American ones. It was only last month, when Hormuz tentatively reopened, that European shares began to catch up.
paragraphs.zh: 以一个案例来说明，看看伊朗战争导致霍尔木兹海峡关闭后发生了什么。一位欧洲股权策略师说，在此之前，她大部分时间都在接听国际投资者的电话，这些投资者担心自己在美国市场上的敞口过高，想要分散投资。资本流入欧洲股票基金的速度比一段时间以来都要快。等到炮火开始落下，电话瞬间减少，资本流入几乎完全枯竭。股价几乎在所有地区都大幅下跌，但欧洲公司的股价恢复比美国公司慢得多。直到上个月霍尔木兹海峡暂时重新开放，欧洲股价才开始追赶。

paragraphs.en: The irony is that the strait’s closure probably boosted Europe’s corporate profits, even as it threatened the continent’s economy—and may do so again as the war resumes. Morgan Stanley finds that 20% of the earnings underlying the MSCI Europe share index come from firms that would have benefited from the spike in commodity prices, such as energy and chemicals producers. The investment bank also estimates that only 10% of European earnings (from car- and foodmakers, for example) would have been harmed by the supply-chain disruption. Investors nevertheless see the war as a cue to shun European stocks en masse.
paragraphs.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，海峡关闭很可能反而推高了欧洲企业的利润，尽管它威胁到了欧洲经济——随着战争重启，这种情况可能再次发生。摩根士丹利发现，MSCI 欧洲股票指数所代表的收益中约有20%来自那些会受益于商品价格飙升的公司，例如能源和化工生产商。该投资银行还估计，只有约10%的欧洲收益（例如汽车制造商和食品制造商）会受到供应链中断的不利影响。尽管如此，投资者仍将这场战争视为整体回避欧洲股票的信号。

paragraphs.en: Europe’s sclerotic growth, similarly, need not condemn its firms’ bottom lines. Taken in aggregate, over half their revenue comes from outside Europe’s developed economies; weighted by firms’ market value (as share indices tend to be) this rises to 60%. No other similarly sized region has stock markets that are so exposed to countries beyond its borders. Investors in European share indices are betting more on the rest of the world than they are on Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 欧洲的低迷增长，同样不必然意味着其企业的利润底线注定受损。总体来看，它们的收入中超过一半来自欧洲发达经济体之外；按企业市值加权（股指通常如此）后，这一比例上升到60%。没有其他体量相当的地区，其股市对本土边界之外国家的依赖性如此之高。投资于欧洲股票指数的投资者，对世界其他地区的押注其实比对欧洲本身更多。

paragraphs.en: Even the burst of inflation brought on by the Iran war should be no bad thing for European shares. Around 40% of them by market value represent firms that derive earnings from real assets, from commodities and machinery to the semiconductors that power AI. Such assets tend to perform well when consumer prices are rising. Another 20% or so of Europe’s stock market is made up of banking shares, which also do well when prices (and hence interest rates and lending margins) are on the way up.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗战争引发的通胀高涨也不应对欧洲股票成为坏事。按市值计算，大约40%的欧洲股票市值来自从事有形资产的公司，覆盖从大宗商品和机械到驱动人工智能的半导体等领域。这类资产在消费价格上升时往往表现良好。欧洲股市中又有约20%是银行股，而银行股在物价上涨（从而带动利率和放贷利差上行）时也通常表现较好。

paragraphs.en: Equity analysts, therefore, are feeling more and more bullish about Europe’s corporate profits, estimating that earnings per share will grow by 17% in 2026. This is admittedly only two-thirds of the growth pencilled in for American stocks, but well above the 9% consensus of two years ago. After a quiet few months European strategists might soon find their phones ringing again. If they do, it may be an idea to remind the investor on the other end that Europe’s stock market is nothing like its economy. ■
paragraphs.zh: 因此，股权分析师对欧洲企业盈利越来越看好，预计2026年的每股收益将增长17%。这当然仅是美国股市预期增长幅度的三分之二，却明显高于两年前的9%共识预估。经过安静了几个月后，欧洲策略师们可能很快就会再次听到电话铃声。如果真的发生，不妨提醒对方那端的投资者：欧洲股市和欧洲经济并不一样。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订阅者可以订阅我们的Opinion电子通讯，里面汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。
